+Add to Mast
Set your engagement mode in 30 seconds.
Mast now adapts the admin to how you work. We can pre-fill based on your existing setup — you'll review before anything saves.

Dashboard

⚙️
Create renewal reminders from your compliance items
You’ve tracked licenses, insurance, or certifications. We can turn those expiry dates into reminders you’ll see in Advisor and receive by email.

Trips

Loading trips...

Revenue

Select a date range and click Load Sales to view Square transactions.
Coming Soon
Revenue
Sales sync from Square, Stripe, Etsy, and manual sources — all processors in one view with daily and period summaries.
Revenue data will be wired from the existing sync view in a future session.
Coming Soon
Expenses
Bank-connected expense tracking via Plaid. Categorize, approve, and export transactions. Linked to your business accounts.
Expense data will be wired from the existing Plaid view in a future session.
Roadmap
P&L
Income statement assembled from your revenue, expenses, and cost of goods. Period selector with month, quarter, and year views. Export to CSV or PDF.
Requires Revenue and Expenses to be fully wired before P&L can be calculated.
Roadmap
Cash Flow
Running cash balance across all connected accounts. Shows inflows (sales, deposits) and outflows (expenses, payroll) on a rolling timeline.
Will pull from Revenue and Expenses once both are live.
Roadmap
Accounts Receivable
Outstanding invoices, wholesale balances, and commission consignments owed to you. Aging report with 30/60/90-day buckets.
Will surface data from Orders, Wholesale, and Galleries modules.
Roadmap
Accounts Payable
Vendor bills, materials purchases, and recurring obligations. Track what you owe and when it's due.
Will surface data from Procurement and Expenses modules.
Roadmap
Tax
Quarterly estimated tax calculations based on net income. Sales tax collected by jurisdiction. Schedule C prep summary for sole proprietors.
Replaces TaxJar for sub-$2M businesses. Period selector and export coming.
Roadmap
Reports
Pre-built financial reports: income statement, mileage summary, expense export by category, show-by-show profitability, and Schedule C prep package.
Reports are generated from live Finance data once Revenue and Expenses are wired.

Settings

Business setupActive

Defines what kind of business Mast is configured for. This is load-bearing — it controls which modules appear in your sidebar by default, and most other settings depend on this choice. Use this section to change your business type; we’ll preview the impact before applying.

GitHub personal access tokenNot configured

Required only when your image storage backend is GitHub. Uploads from the gallery write directly to your GitHub repo — the token needs repo scope. If you’re not using GitHub for image storage, this tab won’t apply and the section will be hidden.

Active Mast pagesRead-only

Read-only summary of which Mast-hosted pages are currently live for this tenant. Use this to confirm what the public sees. To change which feature pages are enabled, use the Feature pages tab (only shown for non-storefront tenants).

Loading…
Feature pagesPick pages

Choose which Mast feature pages are active for your site. These pages are reachable at your Mast domain even without a full storefront. Useful for tenants who only want a subset (e.g. blog + commissions but no shop).

Public feedback widgetOff

Adds a floating “Send feedback” button to your public site so visitors can submit comments. Submissions land in your admin Engagement Inbox. Off by default.

Admin preferencesPer-admin

Settings for the admin app itself — what you see while working in Mast. These don’t affect your customers or your public site at all. Dark mode is a per-device visual preference; Testing Mode and Error Reporting are per-tenant.

Your personal preference. Each user on this tenant can set their own — flip it here or from the avatar menu in the top-right.

Hide the rewards coins / token counter from the top-right header and the account menu. Display-only — doesn’t affect your balance or usage. Applies to everyone on this tenant.

When on, Mast automatically reports JS errors, failed asset loads, and spinner timeouts back to the platform. Helps us find and fix problems you might not notice. No manual action required — data is anonymized.

Adds a Testing Mode panel to your admin sidebar with guided QA missions. Useful while setting up the site and want a checklist of what to validate. Turn off once you’re live.

AccessibilityPer device

Make Mast easier to see and use. The controls here adjust the admin app itself and are saved on this device. Below them are tips for your computer’s and browser’s own accessibility tools, which work on every site — not just Mast.

Scales all text and controls across the admin app. Larger sizes are easier to read on small or high-resolution laptop screens. Takes effect immediately.

Minimizes animations and transitions throughout the admin app. Mast already follows your operating system’s “reduce motion” setting automatically — turn this on if you want to reduce motion only in Mast.

Looking for dark mode? It lives in Preferences.

More ways to make Mast easier to readYour device

These use your computer’s and browser’s built-in accessibility features. They apply to every website and app, not just Mast.

Zoom the whole page in your browser
Mac: press ⌘ + to zoom in, ⌘ − to zoom out, ⌘ 0 to reset. Windows / Linux: use Ctrl in place of ⌘. Chrome and Edge remember zoom per site, so you can keep Mast enlarged without affecting other sites.
Increase your browser’s default font size
Chrome / Edge: Settings › Appearance › Font size. Safari: Settings › Websites › Page Zoom. Firefox: Settings › General › Fonts & Colors. Because Mast’s text scales with this setting, larger text reflows cleanly instead of just magnifying.
Make everything on your screen bigger (operating system)
Mac: System Settings › Displays › choose a Larger Text scaled resolution. Windows: Settings › Accessibility › Text size, or Display › Scale. Good for small built-in laptop screens.
Use your operating system’s magnifier
Mac: System Settings › Accessibility › Zoom (e.g. hold Ctrl and scroll to magnify under the pointer). Windows: press ⊞ + to open Magnifier. Handy for spot-reading fine print.
Payment processorLoading…

Pick the payment processor that takes money for your storefront and POS. Only one can be active at a time — switching preserves the inactive processor’s credentials so you can flip back without re-entering them. The active processor’s credential block is shown below; saved credentials apply on your next checkout.

Square
Square credentialsNot configured

Save credentials for both environments (sandbox for testing, production for real charges). The Active Environment dropdown decides which set Mast uses at checkout. Tokens are stored in encrypted secret storage — Mast staff never see them.

SANDBOX MODE — Test payments only, no real charges
Sandbox Credentials
Production Credentials
Stripe credentialsNot configured

Paste your Stripe secret + publishable keys. The environment is auto-detected from the key prefix (sk_test_ or sk_live_) so a test key flips the whole storefront into Stripe Test Mode automatically. Keys are stored in encrypted secret storage.

TEST MODE — Using test keys, no real charges
Shipping regions and rates should match your Operations settings — fulfillment modes and service area drive who can check out with shipping.
Shipping label providerNot configured

Choose how shipping labels get generated when you fulfill an order. Manual means you paste tracking numbers from Pirate Ship or any external service. Shippo (API) buys labels and pulls tracking directly inside Mast — the default ship-from location + package presets configured here drive that flow.

Not configured

Only studios with a physical address are shown. Manage studio locations

Common box sizes for quick selection when shipping orders. Presets save automatically.

Shipping rulesConfigured

Pick how shipping is calculated at checkout for retail and wholesale orders. Each track has its own strategy (category flat rate, single flat, percentage of subtotal, or free) plus optional modifiers like “free over $X”. Rules apply on top of whatever label provider you chose — this is what the buyer pays, not what you spend on labels.

$
$
Legal identity & tax IDOptional

Your business’s legal details — legal name, DBAs, entity type, year founded, and federal EIN. All fields are optional; add what you know. The EIN block includes regulatory defensive copy and the CCPA Notice at Collection.

One DBA per line.

Format: XX-XXXXXXX

Sales taxDefaults

Tax rates by US state, applied at checkout based on the customer’s shipping state. Set your nexus state (where you collect tax) and configure rates for applicable states. Multi-state registrations live in Compliance → Tax jurisdictions.

State Rate (%)

Track licenses, insurance, certifications, and multi-state tax jurisdictions in Settings > Compliance — renewal dates, registration IDs, and document uploads.

Create renewal reminders from your compliance items
We’ll read your license, insurance, certification, and tax-filing expiries and create reminders you can manage in Notifications.
LicensesNone

Business licenses, permits, and professional credentials. Track registration IDs, issuer, and renewal dates; upload supporting documents per item.

InsuranceNone

Liability, property, or other business insurance policies. Track carrier, policy number, and effective/expiry dates; upload the certificate of insurance per item.

CertificationsNone

Food-handler, ServSafe, trade, and similar professional certifications. Track the credential name, issuer, and expiry; upload the certificate per item.

Tax jurisdictionsNone

Multi-state sales-tax registrations — one row per state where you’re registered to collect. Sales-tax rate tables remain on Settings > Tax & Legal → Sales tax.

Fulfillment modesConfigured

Declare how customers can take delivery — ship, local pickup, in-person at events, etc. Add one row per mode. The label you type here shows at checkout, and connected channels + the Shipping tab read this list to decide which checkout options to surface.

Connected channels (Shopify / Etsy / Square) and Settings > Shipping read this list when computing checkout options. Removing a mode does not retroactively cancel pending orders.

Service areaConfigured

Tell Mast where you serve customers (local radius, list of regions, nationwide, global). This drives Shipping zones and informs Tax jurisdiction hints in Compliance — both surfaces read from this answer.

Locale & timeConfigured

Timezone is editable here and affects scheduled renewal reminders + ICS feed timestamps. Currency, language, and fiscal-year were set during onboarding and are wizard-managed — the summary below shows the current values; talk to the Advisor or re-run the wizard to change those.

Loading…

Affects scheduled renewal reminders and ICS feed timestamps.

Loading…

Currency, language, and fiscal-year are set during onboarding. To change those, talk to the Advisor or edit via the wizard. Timezone is editable above.

Email providerNot configured

Configure how order receipts, commission notifications, and other emails are sent. Pick a provider, paste credentials if required, and set your From/Owner addresses. Use the Mast-managed default unless you need to send from your own domain.

Mast-Managed Email

1 token per email, sent from

Token balance: —

Gmail Setup

Requires a Gmail App Password. In your Google Account:

  1. Enable 2-Step Verification
  2. Go to Security > App Passwords
  3. Create an app password for "Mail"

Set these secrets in GCP Secret Manager with your tenant prefix:

{prefix}-gmail-user
{prefix}-gmail-app-password
Bring Your Own Resend

Paste a key from resend.com/api-keys. When connected, emails route through your Resend account and don't consume Mast email tokens.

To capture opens, clicks, bounces, and complaints, add this URL as a webhook destination in your Resend dashboard, then paste the signing secret here. Required only if you've connected your own Resend account.

Email triggersDefaults

Control which automated emails are sent to customers. Toggle individual emails on or off. Click any email to preview what your customers will see.

Email Preview
Subject
Select an email to preview.
Business alertsDefaults

Configure which business alerts you receive and how they're delivered. Alerts are evaluated automatically every 4 hours.

Alert historyRead-only

A log of business alerts that have fired recently, with their evaluation results and delivery status. Use this to confirm an alert ran or to debug a missed notification.

Toast preferencesPer device

Customize where confirmation toasts appear, how long they stay visible, and how big they are. Errors are always persistent until dismissed regardless of these settings. Preferences are stored on this device.

Renewals remindersConfigured

Reminders for your licenses, insurance, certifications, and tax jurisdictions. Email is delivered via Resend. In-app reminders appear on your Advisor page.

SMS isn’t supported. Email plus in-app covers the B2B SaaS norm without TCPA / 10DLC overhead.

Days before expiry to fire reminders. Comma-separated whole numbers. Short-fuse is used for domains, tax filings, and channel tokens. Long-fuse is used for licenses, insurance, and certifications.

Subscribe in Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or Outlook to see your renewal expiries on your calendar. Anyone with this URL can view your renewal schedule, so treat it like a private link.

Add to Google Calendar Add to Apple Calendar

Outlook: paste the URL into “Add calendar → Subscribe from web.”

These windows apply unless you override a reminder item individually.

Cadence Source types Default ticks (days before expiry)
Long-fuse License · Insurance · Certification · Other 60, 30, 14, 7, 1
Short-fuse Domain · Tax filing · Channel token 30, 14, 7, 1

12-hour dedupe floor. Short-fuse items continue firing at 0 and +1 post-expiry.

Used for address autocomplete at checkout. Restrict by HTTP referrer in Google Cloud Console.

Connect your Etsy shop to sync orders automatically. Etsy orders appear in the Orders tab with an "Etsy" badge.

From your Etsy Developer app dashboard (Your Apps)

Listed next to the Keystring in your Etsy app. Required by Etsy since Feb 2026 — both are needed to sync.

Connect your Shopify store so Mast can read your catalog and publish product changes back to Shopify. Your access token is stored in encrypted secret storage — Mast staff never see it.

Enter your store's .myshopify.com subdomain, then click Connect Shopify to approve the requested permissions in Shopify — you'll be brought right back here.

.myshopify.com
Advanced: connect with API credentials instead
How to create a custom app (5 minutes)
  1. Log in to your Shopify admin at admin.shopify.com.
  2. Click Settings (gear icon, bottom left) → Apps and sales channels → Develop apps.
  3. If prompted, click Allow custom app development and confirm.
  4. Click Create an app → name it Mast Integration → Create app.
  5. Open the Configuration tab → under Admin API access scopes, click Configure. Check these boxes:
    • read_products, write_products
    • read_product_listings
    • read_inventory, write_inventory
    • read_orders
    • read_locations
    • read_content (optional)
    Click Save.
  6. Open the API credentials tab → click Install app at the top → confirm Install.
  7. Under Admin API access token, click Reveal token once and copy it. It starts with shpat_. You can only view it once.
  8. Paste it into the token field below and click Save & Test.

Create an app in the Shopify Dev Dashboard, then paste the Client ID and Secret below.

Your full .myshopify.com subdomain — find it in your Shopify admin URL or Settings → Domains.

.myshopify.com

Found in your app's Settings → Credentials section.

For stores with an existing custom app that provides a static shpat_ access token.

Just the subdomain — we append .myshopify.com automatically.

.myshopify.com

Found under your custom app's API credentials tab → "API secret key". Used to verify inbound webhooks. Without it, webhooks are accepted unsigned.

Sync day-close journals, AR invoices, vendor bills, and reviewed expenses to QuickBooks. Sandbox V1 only.

Connect a bank account or credit card to automatically import transactions into Finance → Expenses. Access tokens are stored in encrypted secret storage — Mast staff never see them.

Product Information Management

Manage channel sync drift alerts, review the sync audit log, and run integration tests.

Drift review queueLoading…

Pending drift alerts where a connected channel disagrees with Mast on title, description, price, status, or inventory. Resolve each by accepting the channel value, keeping Mast's value, or editing manually.

Loading drift alerts...
Sync audit logRead-only

A read-only audit trail of every sync action between Mast and connected channels (Shopify, Etsy, etc.). Use this to confirm a push landed or to debug a missed update.

Loading sync log...
Integration test harnessDev tools

Read-only and reversible-write tests against connected channels. Use these to verify your Shopify credentials, exercise metafield and inventory paths, or seed/clean up sample products. Every call is audit-logged.

Test Harness (Phase 1: read-only)

Runs read-only API calls against your Shopify store. No writes, no changes. Every call is audit-logged.

Seed Sample Products (writes — tagged, reversible)

Creates 3 sample draft products tagged mast-seed-data. Not visible on storefront. Clean up deletes all tagged products.

Phase 2: Metafield Write & Revert

Creates a metafield, verifies, deletes, confirms restored state. Requires seed products.

Phase 3: Product Content Write & Revert

PIM-critical test. Captures title, body_html, vendor, etc.; updates; verifies; reverts; confirms byte-level equality.

Phase 4: Variant Price Write & Revert

Captures variant price, bumps by $1, verifies, reverts, confirms equality.

Phase 5: Inventory Level Delta (+1 / -1)

Adjusts inventory +1, verifies, adjusts -1, confirms restored. Delta-based, race-safe.

Phase 6: Product Image Add & Delete

Uploads a 1x1 PNG, verifies, deletes, confirms image list restored. No external fetch.

Phase 7: Full Product Lifecycle (Create & Delete)

Creates ephemeral draft product, verifies via GET, deletes, confirms 404. No existing data touched.

Phase 8: Webhook Subscription Create & Delete

Creates a test webhook, verifies, deletes, confirms list restored. Shop-level, isolated from product data.

Sync rulesDefaults

Per-field rules that control which Mast→channel or channel→Mast updates apply automatically vs. queue for review. Tune these once your initial sync is clean and you trust a channel's data on specific fields.

Loading rules...
Develop SettingsAtomic widgets

Recipe feedback, drift severity, and repricing thresholds. Toggle and dropdown changes save immediately.

Loading maker settings…
Product SettingsCatalog defaults

Pricing defaults and markup strategy applied across your catalog.

Loading product settings…
Cost-to-Serve DefaultsPortfolio model

Per-ticket and per-return costs used by the Customer Portfolio quadrant model to compute net contribution per customer. Lower these for self-serve customers; raise them for high-touch service models.

Loading cost-to-serve defaults…
Close PolicyAuto-close

When and how monthly periods auto-close. Per-period extensions let you keep a specific month open past the auto-close date (e.g. for a delayed reconciliation).

Loading close policy…
OperatorsLoading…

People who work on production builds in your workshop. Names entered here appear in the operator selector when starting a build, on time-tracking entries, and on labor-cost reports. Add everyone who logs build time.

Studio locationsLoading…

Physical places where you work — home studio, rented space, etc. — with GPS coordinates. Studio Companion uses these to detect when you’re at a known studio vs. a remote event; that controls whether a PoS sale auto-links to the studio or prompts you to tag a show. Detection radius is 500m.

Tap “Add current location” while you’re physically at the studio — your GPS coordinates will be saved automatically.

Inventory locationsLoading…

Storage spots where physical inventory lives — shelves, display cases, containers, etc. Each location gets a QR code for quick scanning during intake, packing, and moves. The “Home” location is created automatically and can’t be archived.

Build media storageFirebase Storage

Where photos captured during production builds are saved. This is read-only platform configuration — build photos go to Firebase Storage under your tenant’s scoped path. Nothing to change here; this tab confirms the backend.

Loading...
IRS mileage ratesLoading…

Standard IRS rates used to compute tax-deductible mileage on business trips. Each rate is year-keyed; when you create a trip, the rate for that year is locked in (so historical trips don’t change when the IRS publishes a new rate). Add a new row each January when the IRS announces the year’s rate.

Trip locationsLoading…

Address book for trip origins and destinations — the named places that show up in the trip pickers (e.g. “Studio”, “Glass Supply Co.”, “Salem Show”). New destinations are auto-added when you log a trip to a new place, so this list grows on its own; use the form below to rename, archive, or add one manually.

Product page display settings moved to My Website → Product page display.

Dashboard CardsPer user

Toggle cards on or off, and drag to reorder. Changes apply to your account only.

Quick Actions CountPer user

Number of quick action tiles to show on the dashboard. Your most-used actions appear first.

Domain settings moved to My Website → Domains.

Ask AI providerMast-managed

Powers the Ask AI features inside Mast admin — cost previews, page-context summaries, the ✨ Ask AI buttons. Default is Mast-managed (we use our platform Anthropic key and deduct tokens from your plan). You can switch to bring your own Anthropic key below to bill Anthropic directly and skip token deduction.

Bring your own Anthropic key

Paste a key from console.anthropic.com. AI calls bypass Mast tokens and bill directly to your Anthropic account. We validate the key against Anthropic before saving.

✓ Connected · —
This month's Ask AI usage
Loading…
AI integrationsMCP & API keys

Let external AI tools (Claude.ai, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) manage your store through natural language. This tab covers (1) the in-page ✨ Ask AI buttons that send page context to your AI tool, (2) API keys for tools that need bearer-token auth instead of OAuth, and (3) the MCP setup instructions and startup prompt.

Loading…

Show ✨ Ask AI buttons across the admin so you can send page context (filters, aggregates, top transactions) to your AI tool. Buttons stay hidden until both prerequisites below are set. This is configured per user — each admin who wants Ask AI sets their own.

Required because the deep-link uses claude:// to open Claude Desktop. (Other AI tools coming later.)

Loading API keys...
Your API Key

Copy this key now — it won't be shown again.

Setup Instructions

Create a new MCP connector called "Mast Tenant MCP" in your AI tool and add the server URL below.

Server URL:
Claude.ai
  1. Go to Settings > Connectors > Add MCP
  2. Name it "Mast Tenant MCP"
  3. Paste the server URL above
  4. Claude.ai supports OAuth — just sign in with your Google account when prompted. No API key needed.
Claude Code

Add to your .mcp.json or Claude Code settings. Uses API key auth:


                
              

            

          

          

            Cursor / VS Code
            

              
Add to your settings.json under MCP servers. Uses API key auth:

              

                

                
              

            

          

          

            Other AI Tools
            

              

                If your AI tool supports MCP connectors, add the server URL above. If it supports OAuth, just sign in — no API key needed. Otherwise, generate an API key above and add it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
              

            

          

        

        
        

          

          
Startup Prompt

          

            After connecting your AI tool, paste this prompt into your project instructions (Claude.ai) or custom instructions (other tools). This teaches your AI assistant how to work with your store.
          

          

            

            
          

          

            Once pasted, just say "Startup Mast" in any conversation to get started.
          

        

        

      

      
      

        

          
Engagement & NavigationOnboarding captured

          
Your engagement mode was captured during onboarding. You can change how Mast looks (surface) and which modules appear in the sidebar here.

        

        

          📝 Bootstrapped setup
          

            Your engagement settings were auto-filled because this tenant pre-dates the onboarding wizard. Review and adjust below.
          

        

        

          ℹ️ No business entity record
          

            Changes here save to the entity record as a draft. Complete the Business Entity wizard to finish onboarding.
          

        

        

          
          

            Controls which import jobs fire and which modules appear. Switching mode re-applies surface defaults.
          

          

          
          

        

        

          
          

            How Mast presents itself. AI-first collapses the sidebar to Dashboard + Manage; UI-first shows only the modules you select below; Hybrid shows the full sidebar with an Advisor banner.
          

          

            
Loading...

          

          

        

        

          
          

            Choose which modules people see in the sidebar. Modules you turn off are hidden from the UI but stay available through the AI assistant. Dashboard and Admin are always shown.
          

          

            
Loading...

          

          

            
            
          

          

        

      

      
      

        

          
          
        


        
        

        
Declared channelsNone

        
Lightweight declaration of where you sell — Instagram, fairs, wholesale rep, etc. Toggle the channels you actually use. Saved to your business entity record as presence.declaredChannels[] and read by reports that need to attribute revenue across surfaces. This is just an inventory; OAuth/sync lives in the Connected tab.


        

          
        


        

          

            
Loading...

          

          

            
          

          

        

        


        
        

        
Connected integrationsNone connected

        
OAuth-authenticated marketplaces with live token + webhook subscriptions. Connecting a platform pulls your catalog and syncs inventory both ways. Each card shows current connection health; reconnect prompts surface here when a token expires.

        
        


          
          

            
⚠️ Channel connections gated for EU tenants

            
Marketplace connectors (Shopify / Etsy / Square) are held pending counsel review of EU DPA subprocessor classification (GDPR Article 28). Once cleared, you'll see the platform cards here. No action required from you.

          


          
          

            

              

                📊
                Channel health
